verdict: Net debt fell from A$849m to A$659m in one quarter (Sep 2025) with no repayments due until FY29 and A$570m of projected cash flow upside at current spot prices; the balance sheet recovery is running materially ahead of the prior debt-stress narrative.

hypothesis.bull: Copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry (A$(1,926)/oz AISC) and Northparkes (A$(2,993)/oz AISC) structurally underwrite group AISC below A$1,800/oz; deleveraging to below A$400m net debt by FY28 allows a materially higher capital return programme; Mungari post-expansion AISC step-down to A$1,982/oz (from A$2,753/oz) confirms the execution track record.
hypothesis.bear: Red Lake persistently operates at A$2,700-2,750/oz AISC (15-17% of group production), acting as a structural drag that cannot be offset by copper credits if gold corrects; Cowal AISC rising to A$2,314/oz in Q1 FY26 due to OPC waste stripping, and group AISC drifting above A$1,700/oz suggests the FY25 A$1,653/oz figure was a floor, not a trend.

evidence.label: Net debt trajectory -- accelerating deleveraging
evidence.finding: Fact: Net debt A$849m at 30 June 2025 (gearing 15%), reduced to A$659m at 30 September 2025 (gearing 11%) after A$170m in early debt repayments. A further A$110m repaid subsequent to quarter end. No debt repayments due until FY29. Total liquidity A$1.3bn including A$525m undrawn revolving credit facility extended to 2028. Remaining debt is US Private Placements maturing FY29, FY31, FY32, FY34, and FY36.

evidence.label: Copper by-product credits -- structural cost advantage
evidence.finding: Fact: Ernest Henry FY25 AISC A$(2,376)/oz; Q1 FY26 A$(1,926)/oz. Northparkes FY25 AISC A$(2,514)/oz; Q1 FY26 A$(2,993)/oz. Copper credits make approximately 20% of group gold production effectively cost-free on an AISC basis. Ernest Henry copper production 47,776t in FY25; Northparkes 28,485t. Combined, these two assets generated A$1,486m revenue in FY25.
evidence.source: EVN FY25 Annual Report, EVN September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Group AISC FY25 and Q1 FY26 trajectory
evidence.finding: Fact: Group AISC A$1,653/oz in FY25 (continuing operations). Q1 FY26 group AISC A$1,724/oz (continuing operations, ex Mt Rawdon which ceased active mining). Company confirmed on track to deliver FY26 production and cost guidance. FY26 group production guidance koz range not explicitly stated in corpus.
evidence.source: EVN FY25 Annual Report, EVN September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Red Lake -- persistent cost drag, improving cash generation
evidence.finding: Fact: Red Lake FY25 AISC A$2,726/oz; Q1 FY26 A$2,703/oz. Gold production 127,632oz in FY25 (approximately 17% of group). Q1 FY26 record net mine cash flow A$39m (second consecutive record). Strategy: margin over volume -- gold grade mined increased 24% to 5.15 g/t in Q1 FY26. Non-current assets in Canada A$1,795.5m (22% of group total). AUD:CAD 5% FX move impacts net assets by A$182.1m.
evidence.source: EVN FY25 Annual Report, EVN September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Mungari mill expansion -- delivered ahead of schedule, under budget
evidence.finding: Fact: Mungari 4.2 mill expansion completed commissioning October 2025, nine months ahead of schedule. Final cost A$212m, 15% below original budget. Processing throughput increased 48% to 919kt in Q1 FY26. AISC stepped down from A$2,753/oz (FY25) to A$1,982/oz (Q1 FY26). Castle Hill open pit provides baseload ore feed for at least 8-10 years.
evidence.source: EVN FY25 Annual Report, EVN September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Cowal -- highest cash contributor, entering OPC waste stripping phase
evidence.finding: Fact: Cowal produced 330,008oz in FY25 (44% of group) with FY25 AISC A$1,752/oz, rising to A$2,314/oz in Q1 FY26. Cost increase driven by planned bi-annual maintenance, weather disruptions, and open pit Stage H completion before waste stripping for Stage I. Cowal accounted for approximately one-third of group operating mine cash flow (A$215m) in Q1 FY26. Board-approved OPC extends open pit life to 2042 at A$430m capex over 7 years.
evidence.source: EVN FY25 Annual Report, EVN September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Cash flow upside at current spot prices
evidence.finding: Fact: At current spot gold and copper prices (basis Q1 FY26 guidance), estimated operating mine cash flow increases from A$2,755m to A$3,325m (A$570m upside), and mine cash flow before major capital from A$2,515m to A$3,085m. Q1 FY26 net mine cash flow A$366m was a record, nearly 20% above the prior record. Every operation delivered positive net mine cash flow.

evidence.label: Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources -- not in corpus
evidence.finding: Information gap: EVN reports Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources annually at 30 June, published the following February. The February 2026 R&R statement is not included in the corpus. Operations include Cowal and Northparkes (NSW), Ernest Henry and Mt Rawdon (QLD -- Mt Rawdon ceasing production FY26), Mungari (WA), Red Lake (Ontario, Canada).

monitoring_trigger: Re-assess at either of two events: (1) February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement -- quantify reserve base, grade, and reserve life across all continuing operations (the largest information gap); (2) December 2025 quarter cash flow result -- assess whether Cowal AISC normalises below A$2,000/oz as Stage H open pit waste stripping completes and whether group net debt falls below A$500m on the current trajectory.
information_gaps: Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources: no JORC-compliant reserve or resource data in corpus. February 2026 annual R&R statement is required to assess reserve life, grade, and mine plan economics for all six operations.
FY26 full-year production guidance (koz range): corpus confirms on track but does not state the explicit group production koz range for FY26.
Red Lake reserve life and mine plan beyond FY26: AISC is persistently high but future production trajectory and reserve life are not disclosed in corpus.

Gold price sensitivity at A$2,200/oz stress case: no formal sensitivity analysis in corpus. At A$2,200/oz AUD, Ernest Henry and Northparkes copper credits would still produce negative AISC, but Cowal, Red Lake, and Mungari margins would compress materially.
Exact royalty rates at Red Lake: Canadian operations show nil royalty in Q1 FY26 cost table but the statutory royalty framework is not described.
corpus_sources_queried: JORC Mineral Resources and Ore Reserves by mine and classification
AISC per ounce by operation for FY25 actual and Q1 FY26 (Cowal, Ernest Henry, Northparkes, Red Lake, Mungari, Mt Rawdon)
Net debt, total debt, cash balance, gearing ratio at June and September 2025; debt maturity profile
FY26 production guidance by mine and trajectory -- Mungari expansion, Red Lake improvement, Northparkes E48 ramp-up
Upcoming catalysts: Mungari commissioning, Cowal OPC, Ernest Henry Bert PFS, Northparkes E22 study
Red Lake jurisdiction: Canadian operations, AUD:CAD FX exposure, royalty rates
Cash flow upside at spot gold and copper prices vs FY26 guidance assumptions
